tool_name: Lifting Lug and Padeye Design Calculator - ASME BTH-1 DNV
category: Lifting & Rigging / Padeye and Lifting Lug Design / Structural Steel
calculation_basis: Lifting lug and padeye design screening using ASME BTH-1, DNV-ST-0378, and AISC-style design checks for pin-hole bearing stress, tearout failure, net-section tension rupture, gross-section yielding, double-plane shear (lug ear), weld group demand at lug-to-base-plate connection, pin bending and shear, cheek plate contribution, combined biaxial stresses at lug hole, sling angle resolution into in-plane and out-of-plane components, fatigue screening (high-cycles), and governing demand-capacity ratio. The output is a preliminary engineering screening calculation and must not replace sealed engineering design, ASME BTH-1 detailed lifting device design, DNV lifting appliance certification, AWS D1.1 weld procedure qualification, load test, proof test, periodic inspection, or competent person review.

inputs.name: Design Code / Standard
inputs.validation_rule: Select governing design code for lifting lug design. Determines safety factors, allowable stress or LRFD factors.
inputs.note: ASME BTH-1 for below-the-hook lifting devices in North America. DNV-ST-0378 for offshore lifting.

inputs.name: Lifting Service Classification

options.label: Severe — > 500,000 cycles (ASME BTH-1 Category D — fatigue required)

inputs.validation_rule: Service class determines safety factors per ASME BTH-1 Table 3-1 or DNV-ST-0378 Table 2-1.
inputs.note: Higher cycle counts require lower allowable stresses and fatigue assessment.

inputs.name: Design Load (Maximum Static Load)
inputs.validation_rule: Maximum static load on lug. Does not include sling angle factor or dynamic factor. Must be positive.
inputs.note: Use maximum lifted weight including rigging hardware but before sling angle magnification.

inputs.name: Sling Angle from Horizontal (θ)
inputs.validation_rule: Angle between the sling leg and the horizontal plane. 0° = horizontal sling, 90° = vertical sling.
inputs.note: Sling angle measured from horizontal. Lower angle = higher tension in sling = higher lug force. Minimum 30° recommended (60° from horizontal = 30° from vertical convention varies).

inputs.name: Sling Out-of-Plane Angle (β)
inputs.validation_rule: Angle of the sling out of the plane of the lug. 0° for in-plane pull.
inputs.note: Out-of-plane loading significantly reduces padeye capacity. Minimize in rigging design.

inputs.name: Dynamic / Impact Factor
inputs.validation_rule: ASME BTH-1: 1.25 for powered hoisting, 1.0 for manual. DNV: 1.30 for offshore lifts.
inputs.note: Applied as multiplier to design load for dynamic effects. Include if lifted by powered crane.

inputs.name: Consequence / Risk Factor
inputs.validation_rule: DNV: 1.0 for personnel not under load, 1.2 for personnel exclusion zone. DNV-OS-H205: 1.3 for critical lifts.
inputs.note: Applied to factored load for critical lifts. For ASME, use Category factor from Table 3-1.

inputs.name: Lug Material Grade

options.label: A36 / S235JR — Fy=250 MPa, Fu=400 MPa
A572 Gr.50 / S355JR — Fy=345 MPa, Fu=450 MPa
A514 Gr.100 / S690QL — Fy=690 MPa, Fu=760 MPa
A709 Gr.50 (Bridge) — Fy=345 MPa, Fu=450 MPa
A709 Gr.70 (Bridge) — Fy=485 MPa, Fu=585 MPa
Custom — Enter Fy and Fu manually

inputs.validation_rule: Select material grade. Applies to both lug plate and cheek plates if same material.
inputs.note: ASME BTH-1 requires minimum notch toughness for Category B and above. Verify CVN requirements per BTH-1 §3.4.

inputs.name: Lug Yield Strength (F_y)
inputs.validation_rule: Minimum specified yield strength per material grade. For A572 Gr.50, Fy=345 MPa.
inputs.note: Use nominal minimum yield per ASTM or EN standard.
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 §25.2 — Yield strength CoV=0.05

inputs.name: Lug Ultimate Tensile Strength (F_u)
inputs.validation_rule: Minimum specified tensile strength per material grade. For A572 Gr.50, Fu=450 MPa.
inputs.note: Used for net-section tension rupture and bearing checks.
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 §25.2 — Ultimate strength CoV=0.04

inputs.name: Pin Hole Diameter (d_h)
inputs.validation_rule: Diameter of the hole machined in the lug for the pin. Typically pin diameter + 1-3 mm clearance.
inputs.note: ASME BTH-1: maximum clearance is 3 mm (1/8 inch) for pin-to-hole fit. Less clearance reduces bearing stress.

inputs.name: Lug Plate Width (w)
inputs.validation_rule: Total width of lug plate perpendicular to load direction. Must exceed d_h + 2× edge distance.
inputs.note: Minimum width typically 2.5 × d_h per ASME BTH-1 or DNV. Width controls net-section and tearout capacity.

inputs.name: Lug Plate Thickness (t)
inputs.validation_rule: Thickness of the main lug plate (or sum of thickness for double-plate padeye). Minimum 6 mm per ASME BTH-1.
inputs.note: Thicker plates increase bearing and tension capacity. Check plate rolling/drilling capability.

inputs.name: Edge Distance — Hole Center to Plate Edge (a)
inputs.validation_rule: Distance from center of pin hole to the nearest edge of lug plate in the load direction (a = L - d_h/2 for symmetrical lug, where L = distance from edge to hole center).
inputs.note: Minimum edge distance per ASME BTH-1 Table 3-2: a_min = 1.5 × d_h typically.

inputs.name: Lug Length from Hole Center to Base (L)
inputs.validation_rule: Distance from pin hole center to the lug base (weld line). Controls bending moment at base weld.
inputs.note: Longer eccentricity increases base weld demand. Keep as short as practical for given rigging configuration.

inputs.name: Cheek Plate Thickness (per side)
inputs.validation_rule: Thickness of one cheek plate (reinforcing plate welded around hole). Set to 0 if no cheek plates.
inputs.note: Cheek plates increase bearing area but the pin transfers load through cheek plates with less efficiency. Typical cheek plates: 0.5 × t.

inputs.name: Number of Cheek Plates
inputs.validation_rule: 0 = no cheek plates, 1 = one side, 2 = both sides.
inputs.note: When 2 cheek plates are used, total effective thickness for bearing = t + 2 × t_c × efficiency_factor.

inputs.name: Cheek Plate Bearing Efficiency Factor
inputs.validation_rule: Factor between 0 and 1 accounting for load distribution inefficiency to cheek plates. DNV/ASME recommend 0.5-0.75.
inputs.note: 0.5 = 50% efficiency (common for ASME). 0.75 for tightly fitted cheek plates ground to match lug contour.

inputs.name: Pin Diameter (d_p)
inputs.validation_rule: Diameter of the pin or clevis pin. Must be ≤ d_h - clearance.
inputs.note: Pin bearing area = d_p × t_effective. Pin bending check uses simple-span or double-shear model.

inputs.name: Pin Yield Strength (F_y_pin)
inputs.validation_rule: Minimum specified yield strength of pin material. Typically higher than lug material.
inputs.note: Common pin materials: AISI 4140 (Fy≈600 MPa), AISI 4340 (Fy≈800 MPa), or stainless for corrosion resistance.
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 §25.2 — Yield strength CoV=0.05

inputs.name: Pin Bearing Length / Clevis Gap Width
inputs.validation_rule: Total bearing length of the pin against the lug. For single-shear: w_b = lug_thickness + gap. For double-shear: w_b = total lug + cheek thickness.
inputs.note: Pin bending span is typically the gap between outer support plates of the clevis or shackle.

inputs.name: Pin Bearing Support Span (g — gap between yoke plates)
inputs.validation_rule: Clear span between the inner faces of the clevis or shackle plates. Controls pin bending moment (simple span).
inputs.note: For symmetric double-shear connections, the pin bending span = g (clevis internal width).

inputs.name: Lug Base Weld Class

options.label: Fillet Weld — All around lug base

inputs.validation_rule: Select weld type at lug-to-base-plate connection.
inputs.note: Fillet welds are common. PJP/CJP provides full-strength connection. AWS D1.1 Table 8.1 applies.

inputs.name: Weld Leg Size (s)
inputs.validation_rule: Fillet weld leg size. Minimum 3 mm (1/8 inch) per AWS D1.1.
inputs.note: Effective throat = 0.707 × s for fillet weld. Two-side welds use both longitudinal weld lengths.

inputs.name: Weld Electrode Ultimate Strength (FEXX)
inputs.validation_rule: Select electrode classification. E70XX is standard for A36/A572 applications.
inputs.note: Weld metal strength must match or exceed base metal strength per AWS D1.1 §5.3.

inputs.name: Base Plate Thickness (t_b)
inputs.validation_rule: Thickness of plate to which the lug is welded. Affects weld heat sink and load distribution.
inputs.note: Base plate must be thick enough to develop full weld strength. Minimum t_b ≥ s + 2 mm for fillet welds.

inputs.name: Base Plate Yield Strength (F_y_base)
inputs.validation_rule: Minimum yield strength of base plate material. Typically matches lug material.
inputs.note: Base plate material should be compatible with lug material for welding (similar strength and chemistry).
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 §25.2 — Yield strength CoV=0.05

inputs.name: ASME BTH-1 Minimum Safety Factor
inputs.validation_rule: ASME BTH-1 Table 3-1: Category A (infrequent) SF=3, Category B (moderate) SF=3, Category C (frequent) SF=4, Category D (severe) SF=5.
inputs.note: SF applies to yield-based checks (gross yielding). For ultimate-based (rupture, bearing), effective SF = SF_t × F_y/F_u or per code table.

inputs.name: ASME BTH-1 Bearing Safety Factor
inputs.validation_rule: ASME BTH-1 Table 3-2: Category A SF=1.1, Category B/C SF=1.25, Category D SF=1.5 (bearing on pins).
inputs.note: Bearing safety factor is typically lower than tension SF because bearing is a less brittle failure mode.

inputs.name: Weld Quality Level

options.label: Standard — AWS D1.1 visual inspection only
Enhanced — AWS D1.1 VT + MT/PT on root pass and finished weld
Premium — AWS D1.1 VT + UT/RT + MT/PT full volumetric NDE

inputs.validation_rule: Weld quality level determines resistance factor on weld capacity.
inputs.note: PREMIUM for critical lifts / personnel-rated lifting devices per ASME B30.20.

inputs.name: Design Fatigue Cycles (if applicable)
inputs.validation_rule: Number of load cycles for fatigue check. Enter 0 for static-only design (no fatigue check).
inputs.note: Fatigue assessment per ASME BTH-1 §3.6 or DNV-RP-C203. Required for service classes C, D and dynamic lifting.

formulas: P_total_N = design_load_N * dynamic_factor * consequence_factor   // [N] Factored design load including dynamic and consequence factors Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.1; DNV-ST-0378 §2.2; Assumptions: Static load multiplied by all applicable load factors
theta_rad = sling_angle_deg * Math.PI / 180   // [rad] Sling in-plane angle in radians Reference: ISO 80000; Assumptions: Standard radian conversion
beta_rad = sling_angle_out_of_plane_deg * Math.PI / 180   // [rad] Sling out-of-plane angle in radians Reference: ISO 80000; Assumptions: Standard radian conversion

P_in_plane_N = P_total_N * Math.cos(beta_rad)   // [N] In-plane component of sling force on lug Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.2; Assumptions: Out-of-plane component resolved separately, load acts at hole center
P_out_of_plane_N = P_total_N * Math.sin(beta_rad)   // [N] Out-of-plane component of sling force on lug Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.2; Assumptions: Out-of-plane loading determined from β angle

P_sling_tension_N = P_total_N / Math.sin(theta_rad) / Math.cos(beta_rad)   // [N] Tension in sling leg from vertical load resolution Reference: DNV-OS-H205 §7.3; Assumptions: Two-leg bridle sling symmetrical about vertical, single sling assumption for lug

P_axial_N = P_in_plane_N * Math.cos(theta_rad)   // [N] Horizontal (axial) component on lug pin hole from sling tension Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.2; Assumptions: Lateral load component creates bending at base
P_transverse_N = P_in_plane_N * Math.sin(theta_rad)   // [N] Vertical (transverse) component on lug pin hole from sling tension Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.2; Assumptions: Primary lifting load direction

t_eff_bearing_mm = lug_thickness_mm + cheek_plate_count * cheek_plate_thickness_mm * cheek_plate_efficiency   // [mm] Effective bearing thickness including cheek plate contribution Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.3; DNV-ST-0378 §5.3; Assumptions: Cheek plates share load with reduced efficiency
A_bearing_mm2 = pin_diameter_mm * t_eff_bearing_mm   // [mm^2] Pin bearing area (projected) Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.3.1; DNV-ST-0378 §5.3; Assumptions: Projected area = pin dia × effective thickness
sigma_bearing_MPa = P_total_N / A_bearing_mm2   // [MPa] Pin-hole bearing stress Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.3.1 Eq 3-3; DNV-ST-0378 §5.3; Assumptions: Uniform bearing stress over projected area
F_bearing_all_MPa = fu_MPa / min_safety_factor_bearing   // [MPa] Allowable bearing stress Reference: ASME BTH-1 Table 3-2; Assumptions: Bearing limit based on ultimate strength divided by bearing SF
UC_bearing = sigma_bearing_MPa / F_bearing_all_MPa   // [dimensionless] Pin-hole bearing utilization ratio Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.3.1; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes

A_net_mm2 = (lug_width_mm - lug_hole_diameter_mm) * lug_thickness_mm   // [mm^2] Net (tension rupture) area at hole section Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.1 Eq 3-5; AISC 360-22 D2; Assumptions: Net section through hole centerline, straight line failure path
A_gross_mm2 = lug_width_mm * lug_thickness_mm   // [mm^2] Gross (yield) area of lug section Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.1; Assumptions: Full cross-sectional area away from hole
sigma_net_MPa = P_total_N / A_net_mm2   // [MPa] Net-section tensile stress at hole Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.1; AISC 360-22 D2; Assumptions: Net area controls for ductile rupture
sigma_gross_MPa = P_total_N / A_gross_mm2   // [MPa] Gross-section tensile stress away from hole Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.1; Assumptions: Gross area check for overall yielding
F_net_all_MPa = fu_MPa / min_safety_factor_tension   // [MPa] Allowable net-section tension stress (rupture) Reference: ASME BTH-1 Table 3-1; Assumptions: Rupture limit state
F_gross_all_MPa = fy_MPa / min_safety_factor_tension   // [MPa] Allowable gross-section tension stress (yielding) Reference: ASME BTH-1 Table 3-1; Assumptions: Yield limit state
UC_net_tension = sigma_net_MPa / F_net_all_MPa   // [dimensionless] Net-section tension rupture utilization Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.1; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes
UC_gross_yield = sigma_gross_MPa / F_gross_all_MPa   // [dimensionless] Gross-section yield utilization Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.1; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes

A_tearout_mm2 = 2 * edge_distance_from_hole_mm * lug_thickness_mm   // [mm^2] Double-plane shear tearout area (both sides of hole) Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.2; Assumptions: Two shear planes each of width = edge_distance_from_hole_mm

sigma_tearout_MPa = P_total_N / A_tearout_mm2   // [MPa] Tearout shear stress Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.2 Eq 3-7; Assumptions: Concentrated load equal to total load resolved into shear on both planes

F_tearout_all_MPa = fu_MPa / (Math.sqrt(3) * min_safety_factor_tension)   // [MPa] Allowable tearout shear stress Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.2; Assumptions: Shear yield = 0.58 × F_y, but code uses 0.577 × F_u / SF typically
UC_tearout = sigma_tearout_MPa / F_tearout_all_MPa   // [dimensionless] Tearout failure utilization ratio Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.2; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes

M_base_Nmm = P_transverse_N * lug_length_mm   // [N·mm] Bending moment at lug base from vertical load Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.6; Assumptions: Cantilever action, eccentricity = L (hole center to base)
P_base_N = P_axial_N   // [N] Axial (horizontal) force at lug base Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.6; Assumptions: Horizontal component transferred as shear or tension in weld
V_base_N = P_transverse_N   // [N] Vertical shear at lug base Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.6; Assumptions: Direct shear transferred to weld
M_base_lat_Nmm = P_out_of_plane_N * lug_length_mm   // [N·mm] Out-of-plane bending moment at lug base Reference: AISC 360-22; Assumptions: Out-of-plane load creates lateral bending at base

A_weld_mm2 = weld_class === 'FILLET_ALL_AROUND' ? 2 * (lug_width_mm + lug_thickness_mm) * weld_size_mm * 0.707 : weld_class === 'FILLET_TWO_SIDES' ? 2 * lug_thickness_mm * weld_size_mm * 0.707 : 0   // [mm^2] Effective weld throat area Reference: AWS D1.1 §2.4; AISC 360-22 J2; Assumptions: Fillet weld throat = 0.707 × leg size, all-around or two-side weld configuration

S_weld_mm3 = weld_class === 'FILLET_ALL_AROUND' ? (2 * (lug_width_mm + lug_thickness_mm) * Math.pow(weld_size_mm * 0.707, 2) / 6 + lug_width_mm * (weld_size_mm * 0.707) * ((lug_thickness_mm + weld_size_mm * 0.707) / 2)) : weld_class === 'FILLET_TWO_SIDES' ? (2 * (weld_size_mm * 0.707) * Math.pow(lug_thickness_mm, 2) / 6) : 0   // [mm^3] Weld group section modulus for bending Reference: AISC Manual §8-2; AWS D1.1 Table 8.1; Assumptions: Simplified weld group elastic section modulus

f_weld_bending_MPa = M_base_Nmm / Math.max(S_weld_mm3, 1)   // [MPa] Bending stress in weld (from vertical moment) Reference: AWS D1.1 §2.4; Assumptions: Linear elastic stress distribution in weld throat
f_weld_axial_MPa = P_base_N / Math.max(A_weld_mm2, 1)   // [MPa] Axial stress in weld (from horizontal load) Reference: AWS D1.1 §2.4; Assumptions: Uniform axial stress on weld throat
f_weld_shear_MPa = V_base_N / Math.max(A_weld_mm2, 1)   // [MPa] Shear stress in weld (from vertical shear) Reference: AWS D1.1 §2.4; Assumptions: Uniform shear stress on weld throat

f_weld_lat_bending_MPa = M_base_lat_Nmm / Math.max(S_weld_mm3, 1)   // [MPa] Out-of-plane bending stress in weld Reference: AWS D1.1 §2.4; Assumptions: Lateral bending stress combined with other weld stresses

f_weld_resultant_MPa = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(f_weld_bending_MPa, 2) + Math.pow(f_weld_axial_MPa, 2) + Math.pow(f_weld_shear_MPa, 2) + Math.pow(f_weld_lat_bending_MPa, 2))   // [MPa] Resultant weld stress (4-component vector sum) Reference: AWS D1.1 §2.4.1.6; Assumptions: Elastic vector addition, instantaneous center of rotation method alternative

F_weld_all_MPa = design_code === 'ASME_BTH_1' ? parseInt(weld_electrode_fu_MPa) * 0.3 / min_safety_factor_tension * 3 : parseInt(weld_electrode_fu_MPa) * 0.3   // [MPa] Allowable weld stress Reference: AWS D1.1 Table 8.1; AISC 360-22 J2-4; Assumptions: Fillet weld allowable = 0.30 × FEXX for tension/shear combined
UC_weld = f_weld_resultant_MPa / Math.max(F_weld_all_MPa, 0.1)   // [dimensionless] Weld group utilization ratio Reference: AWS D1.1 §2.4; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes

M_pin_max_Nmm = P_total_N * pin_support_width_mm / 4   // [N·mm] Maximum pin bending moment (simply supported beam, center point load) Reference: AISC Steel Design Guide; Assumptions: Simple-span between yoke plates, point load at pin center
Z_pin_mm3 = Math.PI * Math.pow(pin_diameter_mm, 3) / 32   // [mm^3] Elastic section modulus of pin (circular section) Reference: ISO 80000; Assumptions: Circular cross-section, solid pin
sigma_pin_bending_MPa = M_pin_max_Nmm / Z_pin_mm3   // [MPa] Pin bending stress Reference: AISC 360-22 F11; Assumptions: Elastic bending stress, circular beam
F_pin_bending_all_MPa = pin_material_fy_MPa / min_safety_factor_tension   // [MPa] Allowable pin bending stress Reference: ASME BTH-1 Table 3-1; Assumptions: Yield-based allowable stress
UC_pin_bending = sigma_pin_bending_MPa / F_pin_bending_all_MPa   // [dimensionless] Pin bending utilization ratio Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.6; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes
A_pin_mm2 = Math.PI * Math.pow(pin_diameter_mm, 2) / 4   // [mm^2] Pin cross-sectional area Reference: ISO 80000; Assumptions: Circular solid pin
tau_pin_shear_MPa = P_total_N / (2 * A_pin_mm2)   // [MPa] Pin double-shear stress Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.2; Assumptions: Double shear, two shear planes
F_pin_shear_all_MPa = pin_material_fy_MPa / (Math.sqrt(3) * min_safety_factor_tension)   // [MPa] Allowable pin shear stress Reference: AISC 360-22; Assumptions: Von Mises shear yield criterion
UC_pin_shear = tau_pin_shear_MPa / F_pin_shear_all_MPa   // [dimensionless] Pin shear utilization ratio Reference: ASME BTH-1; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes
P_double_plane_N = P_total_N   // [N] Double-plane shear load through lug ears Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.2; Assumptions: Total load resolved into two shear planes

A_double_plane_mm2 = 2 * edge_distance_from_hole_mm * t_eff_bearing_mm   // [mm^2] Double-plane shear area (both sides of hole) Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.2; Assumptions: Shear-failure planes extend from hole edge to plate edge on both sides

tau_double_plane_MPa = P_double_plane_N / Math.max(A_double_plane_mm2, 1)   // [MPa] Double-plane shear stress in lug ear Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.2; Assumptions: Uniform shear stress over both failure planes
F_double_plane_all_MPa = fu_MPa / (Math.sqrt(3) * min_safety_factor_tension)   // [MPa] Allowable double-plane shear stress Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.2; Assumptions: Shear based on ultimate strength
UC_double_plane_shear = tau_double_plane_MPa / F_double_plane_all_MPa   // [dimensionless] Double-plane shear utilization in lug ear Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.2; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes

sigma_combined_MPa = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(sigma_net_MPa, 2) + Math.pow(sigma_bearing_MPa, 2) - 2 * sigma_net_MPa * sigma_bearing_MPa * Math.cos(theta_rad))   // [MPa] Combined stress at hole edge (net tension + bearing interaction) Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.7; Assumptions: Vector sum of net-section tension and bearing components

UC_combined_stress = sigma_combined_MPa / (fu_MPa / min_safety_factor_tension)   // [dimensionless] Combined stress interaction utilization at hole Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.7; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes

UC_governing = Math.max(UC_bearing, UC_net_tension, UC_gross_yield, UC_tearout, UC_weld, UC_pin_bending, UC_pin_shear, UC_double_plane_shear, UC_combined_stress)   // [dimensionless] Governing (maximum) utilization ratio across all lug checks Reference: SectorCalc engine rule; Assumptions: All individual UC values calculated correctly
status = UC_governing <= 1 ? 'PASS' : 'FAIL'   // [dimensionless] Overall PASS/FAIL status Reference: ISO 9001 §8.6; Assumptions: All validation checks passed

u_c_UC_governing = UC_governing * Math.sqrt(Math.pow(0.02, 2) + Math.pow(0.01, 2) + Math.pow(0.05, 2) + Math.pow(0.02, 2))   // [dimensionless] Combined standard uncertainty of governing UC Reference: ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM; Assumptions: Independent uncertainty from loads, geometry, material, code safety factor

P_allowable_ASME_N = design_load_N / Math.max(UC_governing, 0.01)   // [N] Allowable load based on governing utilization Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.1; Assumptions: Linear scaling, all UC ratios proportionally

M_base_total_Nmm = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(M_base_Nmm, 2) + Math.pow(M_base_lat_Nmm, 2))   // [N·mm] Resultant bending moment at base (in-plane + out-of-plane) Reference: AISC 360-22; Assumptions: Vector sum of orthogonal moments

rules.message: Design load must be positive. Enter the maximum static lift load.
Pin and hole diameters must be positive.
Pin diameter must be less than hole diameter to allow pin insertion.
Lug width must exceed hole diameter. Minimum width ≈ 2.5 × d_h per ASME BTH-1.
All lug dimensions must be positive.
Weld size and base plate thickness must be positive.
Pin support span must exceed pin diameter. Check clevis / shackle geometry.
Sling angle < 10° from horizontal is extremely shallow. Very high sling tension. Angle must be ≥ 30° (60° from vertical) per standard rigging practice.
Lifting lug/padeye governing utilization exceeds 1.00. Lug design fails. Increase lug thickness, width, or reduce load.
Out-of-plane sling angle exceeds 15°. Out-of-plane loading significantly reduces padeye capacity. Verify rigging to eliminate or minimize out-of-plane pull.

smart_warnings.message: Net-section tension and tearout are the critical ductile failure modes for lifting lugs. Verify these limits govern.
Governing utilization exceeds 0.90. FMEA and independent design review required before fabrication and use.
Edge distance-to-hole-diameter ratio a/d_h < 1.5. ASME BTH-1 Table 3-2 requires minimum edge distance. Increase edge distance or reduce hole diameter.
Bearing stress governs. Consider increasing lug thickness, adding cheek plates, or increasing pin diameter.
Design load includes dynamic factor of DYN. Verify dynamic factor is appropriate for the lifting equipment and lift category per ASME B30.20.
Fatigue cycles entered. Verify fatigue assessment per ASME BTH-1 §3.6 or DNV-RP-C203 for the selected detail category.
Lifting lug safety factor is below ASME BTH-1 minimum of 3.0. Increase lug capacity or reduce safe working load.

engine_rules.error_propagation: ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM Type A/B. Report u_c(UC_governing), input uncertainty table, sensitivity contributors for design load, hole diameter, pin diameter, lug width, lug thickness, edge distance, yield strength, weld size, and sling angle.

fmea.effect: Ovalization of pin hole, progressive deformation, loss of alignment, sudden tearout initiation
fmea.control_measure: Verify bearing stress ≤ allowable per ASME BTH-1 Table 3-2. Increase t or add cheek plates.

fmea.effect: Brittle or ductile rupture through the reduced section at hole, catastrophic failure
fmea.control_measure: Verify net-section stress ≤ allowable per ASME BTH-1 §3.5.1. Increase width or thickness.

fmea.effect: Shear failure of lug ear from hole edge to plate edge, sudden load drop
fmea.control_measure: Verify edge distance ≥ 1.5 × d_h per ASME BTH-1 Table 3-2. Increase edge distance.

fmea.effect: Complete separation of lug from base plate, dropped load
fmea.control_measure: Verify weld resultant stress ≤ allowable per AWS D1.1 §2.4. Increase weld size or add PJP/CJP groove. NDT required.

fmea.effect: Pin plastic deformation, binding in hole, inability to rotate, local stress concentration on lug
fmea.control_measure: Verify pin bending stress ≤ allowable. Increase pin diameter or reduce support span.

fmea.effect: Catastrophic pin fracture at shear plane, complete loss of lifting capacity
fmea.control_measure: Verify pin double-shear stress ≤ allowable. Use higher strength pin material if critical.

fmea.effect: Lateral bending of lug, buckling of thin plate, weld tearing on one side
fmea.control_measure: Minimize out-of-plane angle. Verify combined stress from in-plane + out-of-plane components. Use check plates for stiffening.

fmea.effect: Actual sling tension higher than calculated, overload of lug and pin
fmea.control_measure: Verify sling angle from lift plan. Use inclinometer or measured sling length and hook height. Minimum 30° from horizontal.

reference_code.interface: interface LiftingLugPadeyeInput { design_code: 'ASME_BTH_1'|'DNV_ST_0378'|'EN_1993_AISC'; service_class: 'INFREQUENT'|'MODERATE'|'FREQUENT'|'SEVERE'; design_load_N: number; sling_angle_deg: number; sling_angle_out_of_plane_deg?: number; dynamic_factor: number; consequence_factor?: number; lug_material_grade: string; fy_MPa: number; fu_MPa: number; lug_hole_diameter_mm: number; lug_width_mm: number; lug_thickness_mm: number; edge_distance_from_hole_mm: number; lug_length_mm: number; cheek_plate_thickness_mm?: number; cheek_plate_count?: number; cheek_plate_efficiency?: number; pin_diameter_mm: number; pin_material_fy_MPa: number; pin_bearing_width_mm: number; pin_support_width_mm: number; weld_class: string; weld_size_mm: number; weld_electrode_fu_MPa: string; base_plate_thickness_mm: number; base_plate_fy_MPa: number; min_safety_factor_tension: number; min_safety_factor_bearing: number; weld_quality_level?: string; fatigue_cycles?: number; } interface LiftingLugPadeyeOutput { P_total_N: number; UC_bearing: number; UC_net_tension: number; UC_gross_yield: number; UC_tearout: number; UC_double_plane_shear: number; UC_pin_bending: number; UC_pin_shear: number; UC_weld: number; UC_combined_stress: number; UC_governing: number; status: 'PASS'|'FAIL'; u_c_UC_governing: number; P_allowable_ASME_N: number; }
reference_code.validation: Zod schema. Throw on negative/dimension mismatch, pin ≥ hole, width ≤ hole, zero material properties, missing required field, UC > 1.0, invalid safety factor.
reference_code.testing: Jest. 7 cases: nominal padeye PASS with all ASME checks, double-plane shear critical FAIL, single-shear pin bending PASS, out-of-plane load critical FAIL, edge distance too small WARN, weld only configuration PASS, invalid geometry BLOCK.

tuv_checklist: Dimensional check completed for every formula
Pin double-shear and bending model verified
metadata.calibration_trace: NIST/DKD/PTB traceable pin load cell verification
limitations: Single-pin/single-hole padeye only. Multiple-hole or spreader beam configurations outside scope.
Fatigue assessment is nominal stress screening only. Detailed S-N curve method per DNV-RP-C203 not implemented.
Weld design uses simplified elastic vector method. Instantaneous center of rotation method for eccentric weld groups not implemented.
Shackle/clevis selection and capacity outside scope.
Lift beam, spreader bar, and multiple-point lift systems outside scope.
Base plate bending and anchor bolt design outside scope.
Corrosion allowance, wear, and thermal effects outside scope.
Seismic design, accidental load cases, and progressive collapse outside scope.
